what_it_does: This file is a concatenated, self-contained runtime bundle for ConceptV Studio. It opens with a @ds-bundle manifest (namespace, component list, sourceHashes, inlinedExternals, unexposedExports) and then loads every source module inside an immediately-invoked function wrapped in a try/catch, mounting the root React App and wiring many window-global registries and services.
observations: Starts with a structured @ds-bundle JSON header naming namespace ConceptVDesignSystem_c8f43c and listing components with source paths and hashes.
Each module is wrapped in `try { (() => { ... })(); } catch (e) { __ds_ns.__errors.push(...) }` so failures are captured per source path.

Contains the root App.jsx with state for canvases, persisted storage hooks, global navigation, and a large switch that renders Overview, Inbox, Icons, Colors, Voice, Build, Templates, Registry, Architecture, TypeBuilder, Components, Workshop, Patterns, Imagery, Bridge, Settings, etc.

Includes a full AI layer: ai-registry.js (CV_AI hierarchical registry with providers/behaviours/skills/capabilities/contexts), ai-seed.js, ai-capabilities-canvas.js, ai-glyphic.js, and host-runtime.js / host-serializer.js (CV_HOST environment bridge with sandbox review, File System Access API, native/MCP runtimes, change staging, and source serializers).

Includes several canvas components: AIStudio (image generation/edit/compose/mask/chat), Architecture (registry explanation), Bridge (CV_HOST projection), and Build (multi-agent planner with subtasks, composer, and refinement loop).
Heavy use of transpiled JSX via `React.createElement`, React hooks aliased per file (e.g. `useState_app`, `useState_as`), and inline style objects.
Relies on many window globals: `window.CV_AI`, `window.CV_HOST`, `window.CV_REGISTRY`, `window.CV_ICONS`, `window.cvOpenAI`, `window.cvImageStore`, `window.cvAIPresets`, `window.cvNav`, etc.
The provided content ends abruptly mid-line inside Build.jsx (`background: 'var(--bg-canva`), indicating the file is truncated in this excerpt.
standouts: CV_AI is built as a hierarchical registry mirroring CV_REGISTRY, treating AI capabilities as inheritable, queryable, executable data rather than ad-hoc functions.
CV_HOST introduces explicit runtime resolution: sandbox review floor, browser File System Access API, and native/MCP host, with loud failure and review-first change staging.
The Bridge canvas is described as a pure projection of `CV_HOST.describe()` and CV_AI repo capabilities, with no hand-written status text.
Build.jsx implements a 3-layer planner: brief → plan review → parallel specialist subtasks → composer → refinement loop.
The bundle format captures sourceHashes for every file, suggesting it is meant to be diffed, validated, or hot-reloaded.
conventions: Each source file is loaded as an IIFE inside a try/catch and reports errors to a shared namespace error array.
React hooks are renamed per file to avoid collisions in the concatenated bundle.
JSX is transpiled to explicit `React.createElement` calls with `/*#__PURE__*/` annotations.
Code is heavily commented with section headers explaining the architectural intent.
Registration APIs use a data-first pattern: `register({ id, layer, family, ... })` for types, AI capabilities, host runtimes, and serializers.
Global singletons are attached to `window` and consumed directly by components.
concerns: The file is truncated at the end, so the Build.jsx module and any following modules are incomplete in this excerpt.
Many components assume window globals exist without runtime guards, which could throw in environments where the bundle is loaded out of order.
Inline styles are pervasive, making theming and overrides harder to trace than CSS custom properties.
Some code references unguarded APIs like `window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT.open(d)` without checking presence.
The monolithic bundle mixes presentational components, AI logic, host I/O, and app state, so a failure in one module could affect debugging clarity even though errors are captured per module.
notes: This is the runtime wiring layer: it loads the whole ConceptV Studio app as one bundle, isolates module errors, and projects shared registries into UI canvases.
questions: What build process produces this bundle and how are sourceHashes used at runtime or for cache invalidation?
How is the per-module error array (`__ds_ns.__errors`) surfaced to the user or to the host?
Are the window globals expected to be injected by separate scripts, or are some of them created lazily by this bundle?
How does the bundle handle the unexposedExports listed in the manifest, such as `typeToNode` from core/RenderType.jsx?
Is the truncation at the end of the provided content an artifact of the excerpt, or is the actual file incomplete?
What is the loading order guarantee between this bundle and the React/ReactDOM dependencies it references?
purpose_vs_actual: The manifest frames the file as a component bundle, but the body is a full application runtime including root state, AI registry, host bridge, and canvases — not merely presentational components.
apparent_themes: generative AI as a first-class registry layer
intention_signals: It is trying to make every surface (UI, AI, file system) read from and write through the same hierarchical registries, so adding a capability, type, or token is a registration rather than a code change, and the running UI is a live projection of that catalogue.
novelty: A unified AI registry (CV_AI) and environment host registry (CV_HOST) built in the same shape as the type registry, with explicit sandbox/review/file-access/native-MCP runtime resolution and source serializers that turn registry mutations into literal source patches.
contribution: Provides the live runtime wiring that turns declarative registry entries into an executable app: module loading with per-file error isolation, global namespace wiring, AI and host capability resolution, and the root App that projects registry state into canvases.
summary_for_embedding: _ds_bundle.js is the concatenated ConceptV Studio runtime bundle that loads the root App, registers AI/host/type registries, and renders design-system canvases through a namespace-isolated, error-capturing module loader.
intention_for_embedding: To ship the entire ConceptV Studio runtime as one file where every module is registered, isolated, and projected from shared registries.
